agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-primary model with strict data residency enforcement. Each region (EU, US, APAC) owns its customer and operational data in sovereign datastores, connected via an asynchronous event mesh that replicates only anonymized/aggregated metrics globally. A Global Control Plane provides the unified operations dashboard by consuming projected read-models, never raw PII. Region affinity is enforced at the routing layer (geo-DNS + API gateway) and in the domain model (courier-region invariant). Resilience is achieved through intra-region multi-AZ deployments with a regional failover cell architecture, not cross-region failover, preserving data residency guarantees even during outages.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Geo-DNS + Edge Router","responsibility":"Routes customer requests to their home region based on account-region binding (not IP geolocation alone). Handles TLS termination at regional edge PoPs for <100ms latency.","boundaries":"Stateless. No PII storage. Only holds routing table mapping customer-id-prefix → region."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Authenticates requests, enforces rate limits, validates that the requesting customer belongs to this region. Rejects cross-region requests with a redirect to the correct regional endpoint.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Cannot forward requests to another region's internal services."},{"name":"Order & Delivery Service (per region)","responsibility":"Core domain service handling order placement, delivery assignment, tracking. Enforces courier-region affinity invariant at the aggregate level.","boundaries":"Owns Order and Delivery aggregates. Reads only from regional datastore. Emits domain events to regional event bus."},{"name":"Customer Service (per region)","responsibility":"Manages customer profiles, addresses, payment references. All PII stays within this service's regional boundary.","boundaries":"Strict data residency. No replication outside region. Encrypted at rest with region-local KMS keys."},{"name":"Courier Service (per region)","responsibility":"Manages courier profiles, availability, location tracking. Couriers are hard-bound to a region.","boundaries":"Region-scoped. Courier aggregate contains a region field that is immutable after creation (can only be changed via an explicit region-transfer process)."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Async event backbone within a region. Carries domain events (OrderPlaced, DeliveryAssigned, DeliveryCompleted) for intra-region choreography.","boundaries":"Region-local. Events do not cross region boundaries in raw form."},{"name":"Metrics Projection Service (per region)","responsibility":"Consumes regional domain events and produces anonymized, aggregated metric projections (order counts, delivery times, SLA compliance, courier utilization). Publishes these to the Global Event Mesh.","boundaries":"One-way gate: reads rich regional events, emits only aggregated metrics with no PII. This is the single authorized cross-region data export point."},{"name":"Global Event Mesh","responsibility":"Lightweight cross-region pub/sub layer carrying only anonymized metric events. Backed by a global messaging service (e.g., Kafka MirrorMaker with topic filtering, or a managed global pub/sub like Google Pub/Sub or AWS SNS cross-region).","boundaries":"Carries only metric events. PII is structurally excluded by the Metrics Projection Service contract."},{"name":"Global Control Plane & Dashboard","responsibility":"Consumes aggregated metrics from all regions, materializes a global read-model, and serves the operations dashboard. Provides global config distribution (feature flags, pricing rules).","boundaries":"Read-only for operational data. Config writes flow downstream to regions. Deployed multi-region itself (active-active) but holds no customer PII."},{"name":"Regional Failover Cell","responsibility":"Each region is divided into 2-3 availability zones forming a failover cell. If one AZ fails, traffic shifts to surviving AZs within the same region. Provides degraded-mode capability via pre-cached static content and a read-only order status cache.","boundaries":"Strictly intra-region. No cross-region failover for customer-facing services, preserving data residency."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Geo-DNS + Edge Router","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"HTTPS with SNI routing. Header: X-Home-Region derived from customer account binding.","coupling_type":"loose — routing table updated asynchronously from customer registration events"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Order & Delivery Service","contract":"REST/gRPC. Validated JWT with region claim. Gateway rejects if region claim ≠ deployed region.","coupling_type":"runtime — synchronous request/response within region"},{"from":"Order & Delivery Service","to":"Courier Service","contract":"Synchronous query for available couriers filtered by region + location. Command to assign courier checks region match invariant.","coupling_type":"runtime — synchronous within region, domain coupling on region invariant"},{"from":"Order & Delivery Service","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Publishes OrderPlaced, DeliveryAssigned, DeliveryCompleted events. Schema-registry enforced Avro/Protobuf.","coupling_type":"async — fire and forget, schema-coupled"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Metrics Projection Service","contract":"Subscribes to all domain events. Applies PII-stripping transformation and aggregation windows (5s tumbling).","coupling_type":"async — consumer-driven, no backpressure to producers"},{"from":"Metrics Projection Service","to":"Global Event Mesh","contract":"Publishes RegionMetricsUpdated events containing: region_id, order_count, avg_delivery_time, sla_compliance_pct, active_couriers. No PII fields.","coupling_type":"async — eventual consistency, ~5-10s lag acceptable"},{"from":"Global Event Mesh","to":"Global Control Plane & Dashboard","contract":"Subscribes to RegionMetricsUpdated from all regions. Materializes unified dashboard view.","coupling_type":"async — read-model projection, tolerant of temporary lag"},{"from":"Global Control Plane & Dashboard","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"Config push via Global Event Mesh: feature flags, pricing rules, operational commands. Regions pull and apply.","coupling_type":"async — regions can operate independently if global plane is down (cached config)"}],"data_flow":"Customer request → Geo-DNS resolves to home region edge → Regional API Gateway authenticates and validates region affinity → Request handled entirely within regional services (Order, Customer, Courier) using regional datastore → Domain events emitted to Regional Event Bus → Metrics Projection Service strips PII, aggregates, and publishes to Global Event Mesh → Global Dashboard materializes cross-region view. Data residency is enforced structurally: PII never enters the Global Event Mesh. Cross-region data flow is unidirectional (region → global) and contains only aggregated metrics.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is a self-contained cell with its own data, compute, and event bus. Blast radius of failures is contained to one cell. Cells share nothing except anonymized metrics via the global mesh."},{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty by Construction","rationale":"Rather than relying on policy-based controls (which can be misconfigured), the architecture structurally prevents PII from crossing region boundaries. The Metrics Projection Service acts as a one-way data diode."},{"pattern":"CQRS with Regional Write / Global Read","rationale":"Writes are strictly regional (command side). The global dashboard is a read-model projected from anonymized events. This cleanly separates data residency concerns from global observability needs."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Anti-Corruption Layer","rationale":"The Metrics Projection Service is an ACL between the rich regional domain model and the global metrics model. It translates and filters, preventing the global layer from being coupled to regional domain internals."},{"pattern":"Degraded Mode via Graceful Degradation","rationale":"Regional failover uses intra-region AZ redundancy plus a read-only cache layer. When primary write path is down, customers can still view order status (cached) and see estimated times. New orders queue locally and process when service recovers."},{"pattern":"Geo-Affinity Enforcement in Domain Model","rationale":"The courier-region constraint is modeled as a domain invariant inside the Delivery aggregate, not as a downstream validation. This makes violations impossible rather than merely detectable."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, contains line items, delivery requirements, region binding)","Delivery (root: deliveryId, enforces courier-region invariant, tracks lifecycle)"]},{"name":"Customer Management","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: customerId, contains profile, addresses, region assignment — immutable region field)"]},{"name":"Courier Management","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Courier (root: courierId, contains profile, availability, certifications, immutable region binding)","CourierShift (root: shiftId, tracks active shift, location updates)"]},{"name":"Global Operations","classification":"generic","aggregates":["RegionMetrics (root: regionId + window, contains aggregated KPIs — no identity, pure value projection)","OperationalAlert (root: alertId, threshold-based alerts on metric anomalies)"]},{"name":"Billing & Payments","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Invoice (root: invoiceId, region-scoped, references regional payment processor)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Courier Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management requests courier assignment; Courier Management supplies availability. Courier Management can evolve its internal model independently as long as the availability query contract is maintained."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Order Management emits rich domain events. The Metrics Projection ACL translates these into anonymized metric events before they reach Global Operations. Global Operations never sees the Order model directly."},{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Conformist. Order Management conforms to Customer Management's customer-id and region-assignment model. Customer region is treated as an immutable fact by Order Management."},{"upstream":"Global Operations","downstream":"All Regional Contexts","relationship":"Open Host Service / Published Language. Global Operations publishes config and operational commands via a well-defined schema. Regional contexts consume what they need."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"No cross-region failover for customer-facing services","gains":"Absolute data residency guarantee. No risk of PII leaking during failover. Simpler compliance posture for GDPR/CCPA audits.","sacrifices":"If an entire region goes down (all AZs), customers in that region lose write capability until recovery. They get degraded read-only mode, not full service from another region.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover with encrypted PII transfer — rejected because it creates compliance risk and complexity around key management across jurisdictions","Active-active multi-region with shared database — rejected as fundamentally incompatible with data residency requirements"]},{"decision":"Aggregated metrics only in global mesh (no raw events)","gains":"Structural PII protection. Compliance by construction rather than by policy. Simpler global mesh — lower bandwidth, lower cost.","sacrifices":"Global dashboard cannot drill down to individual order or customer level. Operators needing customer-specific investigation must connect to the specific region's systems.","alternatives_considered":["Tokenized PII in global events — rejected because tokenization schemes still constitute personal data under GDPR if the mapping exists","Full event replication with field-level encryption — rejected due to key management complexity across regions"]},{"decision":"Courier region as immutable domain invariant","gains":"Eliminates an entire class of bugs (cross-region assignment). Makes compliance provable at the domain level.","sacrifices":"Couriers expanding to new regions require a formal region-transfer or multi-region registration process, adding operational friction.","alternatives_considered":["Soft region preference with validation layer — rejected because validation can be bypassed or misconfigured; invariants are safer than validations"]},{"decision":"Cell-based architecture over shared-nothing microservices","gains":"Clear blast radius. Simple mental model. Each cell is independently deployable and testable.","sacrifices":"Some code duplication across cells. Deploying a new feature requires rolling it out to each cell independently.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with data-layer sharding — rejected because it conflates deployment boundaries with data boundaries"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, Latin America)","evolution_path":"Stamp out a new cell: deploy regional services, create regional datastore, configure Geo-DNS entry, connect Metrics Projection Service to Global Event Mesh. No changes to existing regions required."},{"area":"Evolving compliance requirements","evolution_path":"Each region's data handling is independent. If APAC introduces new data residency rules, only the APAC cell's Customer Service and data layer need modification."},{"area":"Global dashboard capabilities","evolution_path":"The Metrics Projection Service contract can be enriched to expose more aggregated dimensions without touching regional services. New metric types are additive."},{"area":"Regional service evolution","evolution_path":"Each region can independently deploy service updates. A new delivery algorithm can be tested in APAC before rolling to EU/US. Cell independence enables regional experimentation."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer-to-region binding","reason":"A customer's region assignment is immutable by design. Migrating a customer between regions requires a deliberate, audited data migration process — this is intentionally hard because getting it wrong violates data residency law."},{"area":"Courier-to-region binding","reason":"Enforced as a domain invariant. Cannot be loosened without redesigning the Delivery aggregate's core invariant checking."},{"area":"PII boundary at Metrics Projection Service","reason":"This is the single authorized cross-region data gate. Any proposal to send more data globally must pass through this chokepoint. This rigidity is a feature — it makes compliance auditable."},{"area":"Event schema contracts","reason":"Regional Event Bus and Global Event Mesh use schema-registry-enforced contracts. Schema evolution must be backward-compatible (Avro/Protobuf compatibility rules). Breaking changes require coordinated migration."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Global Event Mesh becomes a bottleneck or single point of failure for the dashboard","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard operates on cached materialized views. If the mesh is down, dashboard shows stale-but-available data with a staleness indicator. Mesh itself should be multi-AZ within its hosting region."},{"risk":"Clock skew across regions causes metric aggregation inconsistencies","severity":"low","mitigation":"Use event-time (not processing-time) watermarks in the Metrics Projection Service. Accept that global dashboard shows eventually consistent data with a documented SLA (e.g., <30s lag)."},{"risk":"Regional outage exceeds intra-region redundancy (full region loss)","severity":"high","mitigation":"This is an accepted risk given data residency constraints. Mitigation: multi-AZ within region (3 AZs minimum), automated AZ failover, pre-warmed standby capacity. RTO target: <5 minutes for AZ failover. Full region loss is treated as a disaster recovery scenario with manual intervention."},{"risk":"Compliance drift — new regulations require architectural changes","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Cell architecture isolates regulatory impact to affected region. Maintain a compliance-as-code framework that validates data flow boundaries in CI/CD (e.g., integration tests that assert no PII in global mesh events)."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing 3+ independent cells","severity":"high","mitigation":"Invest in a unified deployment pipeline (GitOps with per-region overlays), centralized logging/alerting aggregation (with PII filtering), and infrastructure-as-code templates for cell stamping. Consider a Platform Engineering team to own cross-cell tooling."},{"risk":"Latency budget (<100ms) exceeded due to regional service chain depth","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Keep synchronous call chains shallow (max 2 hops: Gateway → Service → Datastore). Use connection pooling, regional CDN for static assets, and read replicas within region. Load test each region independently against the 100ms P99 target."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Refactor existing EU monolith into the cell architecture pattern — extract Customer, Order, Courier services with clear boundaries, add Regional Event Bus, build Metrics Projection Service. (2) Deploy US cell as a greenfield stamp-out of the EU cell template, with US-specific config (payment processors, compliance rules). (3) Deploy APAC cell. (4) Build Global Control Plane and Dashboard last, once the event mesh is proven with 2 regions. This phasing de-risks the migration by validating the cell pattern in the existing region before expanding.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infrastructure/terraform/modules/regional-cell/ — reusable Terraform module for stamping out a complete regional cell","infrastructure/terraform/environments/{eu,us,apac}/ — region-specific variable files and overrides","services/api-gateway/middleware/region-affinity-validator.ts — middleware that validates JWT region claim matches deployed region","services/order/domain/delivery-aggregate.ts — Delivery aggregate with courier-region invariant enforcement","services/metrics-projection/transformers/pii-stripper.ts — transformation layer that removes all PII fields from domain events before global publication","services/metrics-projection/publishers/global-mesh-publisher.ts — publishes anonymized metrics to the Global Event Mesh","services/global-dashboard/projections/region-metrics-materializer.ts — materializes global read-model from cross-region metric events","infrastructure/event-schemas/ — Avro/Protobuf schema definitions for regional and global events, with schema registry config","tests/compliance/no-pii-in-global-mesh.test.ts — integration test asserting that no PII fields appear in global mesh events","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md — Architecture Decision Record documenting the cell-based approach and data residency strategy"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. CQRS for separating regional writes from global reads. Event-driven with ACL for cross-region data flow. Domain invariants (not validation layers) for compliance-critical rules. Infrastructure-as-code with reusable modules for cell stamping. Schema registry for event contract governance.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do not use IP geolocation alone for region routing — customers traveling abroad must still hit their home region. Use account-level region binding.","Do not replicate raw domain events across regions — even encrypted PII creates compliance risk if decryption keys could theoretically be accessed cross-region.","Do not build cross-region failover for customer services — it fundamentally conflicts with data residency. Invest in intra-region resilience instead.","Do not let the Global Dashboard query regional databases directly — this creates a coupling and compliance backdoor. All global data access goes through the Metrics Projection ACL.","Do not share a single database across regions with row-level filtering — network latency will violate the 100ms target and a misconfigured filter becomes a data breach.","Do not treat courier region assignment as a soft constraint — model it as a hard invariant. Soft constraints get overridden under operational pressure."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via structural boundaries (well-established pattern in regulated industries)","Cell-based architecture for regional isolation (proven at scale by AWS, Netflix, etc.)","CQRS with anonymized projection for global dashboard (clean separation of concerns)","Courier-region invariant as domain-level enforcement (standard DDD practice)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget feasibility (<100ms P99) depends on cloud provider AZ topology and service implementation details — requires load testing to validate","Global Event Mesh technology choice (Kafka MirrorMaker vs. managed pub/sub) depends on existing infrastructure and team expertise","Degraded mode UX during regional AZ failover — exact capabilities depend on what can be meaningfully cached and served read-only"],"assumptions":["The existing EU system can be decomposed into the proposed service boundaries without discovering hidden coupling that would require a different decomposition","Three cloud regions (EU: eu-west-1, US: us-east-1, APAC: ap-southeast-1) provide sufficient coverage for the customer base","The 5-10 second lag for global dashboard metrics is acceptable to operations teams","Customer-to-region binding is 1:1 (a customer belongs to exactly one region, not multiple)"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics architecture using a region-primary model with strict data residency enforcement. Each region (EU, US, APAC) owns its customer and operational data in sovereign datastores, connected via an asynchronous event mesh that replicates only anonymized/aggregated metrics globally. A Global Control Plane provides the unified operations dashboard by consuming projected read-models, never raw PII. Region affinity is enforced at the routing layer (geo-DNS + API
